keyPointsSummary: 'Want to' shows present desire, 'Wanted to' shows past desire
Same agreement rules as 'want' — wants for he/she/it
Negative: do not want to / did not want to
This is a combined revision of Days 11 and 12 with 'to + verb' focus

quickQuiz.answer: He wants to become a doctor.
He wanted to become a doctor.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं यह किताब पढ़ना चाहता हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I want to read this book.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं यह किताब पढ़ना चाहता था।
quickQuiz.answer: I wanted to read this book.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह यह नौकरी करना चाहती है।
quickQuiz.answer: She wants to do this job.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह यह नौकरी करना चाहती थी।
quickQuiz.answer: She wanted to do this job.

mustRememberRules: 'Want to' shows present desire, 'Wanted to' shows past desire
Same agreement rules as 'want' — wants for he/she/it
Negative: do not want to / did not want to
This is a combined revision of Days 11 and 12 with 'to + verb' focus
